title: spergularia diandra var. tenuifolia
intro: Spergularia diandra var. tenuifolia, also known as Spurry or Lesser Spurrey, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Spergularia diandra var. tenuifolia include its ability to treat urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other renal issues, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be cautious and under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with medications.
